items.heading: Introdcution
items.text: The Security report in the Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) provides insights into the security status of enrolled devices. It helps you monitor and fix common risks such as missing updates, encryption status, and system protection settings. This report supports proactive security management by showing risk areas, recommending actions, and helping improve your organization’s security score. The data appears in the following sections:

items.heading: Security Score
items.text: This section shows an overview of the security health across your device fleet.

items.heading: Top Security Issues
items.text: This horizontal bar chart shows the most common security issues. Each bar shows how many devices have that issue. Common issues include: Tip: Hover over each bar to see the exact number of affected devices.

items.heading: Top Security Issue – Details Page
items.text: Clicking a bar in the Top Security Issues chart opens a new page focused on that issue. This helps IT teams understand the impact and plan fixes. Details on the page include: Click a card to view more details and how to fix it. Column Description Serial Number A unique device ID. Click to view more. Device Name A user-assigned name of the device. Last Signed-In User The username or ID of the last signed-in user. Warranty State The warranty status, such as In warranty, Out of warranty, or Unknown. Security Score A numberic value indicating the security health of the device. Missing Security OS Updates The count of missing "Windows security updates." Security Encryption Status Indicates if disk encryption is enabled. Use the search box at the top of each column to filter by device name, serial number, etc.

items.heading: Security Over Time
items.text: This chart shows how your security score changed over time. The X-axis shows time (days or weeks), and the Y-axis shows the number of devices grouped by score category. Categories: Each colored line shows the number of devices in that category over time. IT admins can: Click a point in the chart to view device details for that date. Note: Filters in the main report do not affect this chart.

items.heading: Security Changes Since Last Week
items.text: This section shows how issues have changed in the past 7 days. It helps IT teams track improvements and spot new risks. Each row in the table shows: The report shows the date range at the top. Note: Use this table to track weekly progress and plan next steps.

items.heading: Security Changes – Details Page
items.text: Click an arrow in the chart to open a page with full details about that issue. Click a card to view detailed steps. Unique device ID. Click to view more. Hostname or label. Most recent user. Status like In warranty, Out of warranty, or Unknown. Device’s security health score. Count of missing updates. Shows if disk encryption is on. Use search boxes to narrow down results.

items.heading: Security by Model
items.text: This chart ranks device models based on their security performance: Use this to spot models with frequent issues and plan updates. Tip: Use this data to update firmware or apply model-specific security policies.

items.heading: Security by Model – Details Page
items.text: Click a bar to view detailed information about that model’s issues. Click a card for full instructions. Unique ID. Recent user ID. Warranty status. Score showing security level. Number of missing updates. Shows if encryption is enabled. Use the search bar to filter the list.

items.heading: Introduction
items.text: The System Health report provides visibility into the overall health of your organization’s devices by evaluating hardware-related indicators, such as battery and component statuses. Usng this data, you can identify devices with potential hardware risks and take remedial action to extend the lifespans devices. This data is crucial for proactive fleet management and device maintenance strategies. The insights are visualized through various widgets and charts on a single dashboard.

items.heading: System Health Score
items.text: This section displays the overall System Health Score for the fleet, based on underlying hardware performance indicators.

items.heading: Top System Health Issues
items.text: This section shows the most common issues affecting the system health. The horizontal bar shows the count of devices impacted by each issue. For example, “Battery health issue” has impact 7 devices.

items.heading: System Health Over Time
items.text: A line chart to track the System Health Score improvements or regressions in system performance over time. The X-axis shows the date range and the Y-axis shows the score and number of devices To interpret the chart, look at the green, orange, and red lines across the timeline. For example, if you see the green line rising or staying flat at the top while the orange and red lines remain close to the bottom, it indicates that most devices consistently delivered a great experience, with minimal issues. The chart is not impacted by dashboard filters.

items.heading: System Health Changes Since Last Week
items.text: This section compares the current week's health status with the previous week. The current week is dispalyed on the top with an info icon. If there is insufficient data, the chart shows: “No data to display."

items.heading: System Health by Model
items.text: This bar chart displays system health by device models to help you identiy trends across models. Each bar shows the number of devices in each health category. For exampe Great or Fair health. For example:

items.heading: Network Health Report
items.text: The Network Health report provides a comprehensive view of network stability and connection quality across enrolled devices in your organization. Thiis report enables IT teams to monitor key metrics, such as connection reliability, signal strength, and authentication types. This data is essential for identifying recurring network issues, tracking improvements, and implementing corrective actions. This report includes both high-level summaries and detailed device-level data to support proactive troubleshooting and performance optimization. Data is presented across multiple sections, such as Network Health Score, Top Network Health Issues, Network Issues Since Last Week, and Network Authentication Types. Note: In Windows 11, if Location Services is turned off, Windows blocks access to location-sensitive Wi-Fi data such as signal strength (RSSI) and BSSID. As a result, these values can appear as blank or unavailable in WXP. This restriction is enforced by the operating system and cannot be bypassed. Basic network data, such as SSID, IP information, and adapter details, remains available. To collect full Wi-Fi telemetry, enable Location Services in Settings> Privacy & security > Location, or through Group Policy.

items.heading: Reviewing Network Health Score
items.text: The Network Health Score shows an overall score of network health along with a chart indicating performance over time. Each trend line in the chart represents how many devices fall into each category over time. Note: This chart is not impacted by any filters applied to the report.

items.heading: Top Network Health Issues
items.text: A horizontal bar chart showing the most common network issues detected across the fleet. Each bar represents the number of devices affected by a specific issue, such as: Note: Network disconnections - A network disconnection refers to an event where a device unexpectedly loses its connection to the network. This could result from unstable Wi-Fi signals, router issues, incorrect configurations, or authentication failures. Frequent disconnections can severely impact productivity and user experience. Data will not be collected if a network disconnect happens due to the following scenarios:

items.heading: Device Utilization Report
items.text: The Device Utilization report provides insights into CPU and memory usage across all enrolled devices in your organization. It helps IT teams to monitor resource consumption trends, detect performance bottlenecks, and identify systems that are underutilized or overutilized. These insights support informed decisions on workload distribution, hardware upgrades, and software optimization. The data is displayed across multiple tabs: Summary, By Manufacture Year, Windows Software Performance, Mac OS Software Performance, and Details.

items.heading: Summary
items.text: This tab includes two area charts that show overall trends in CPU and memory usage over time. These charts are useful to track performance fluctuations over time and identify long-term patterns in device resource usage. Hovering over a data point in the CPU Utilization Summary or Memory Utilization Summary charts shows a breakdown of the utilization for that specific date.

items.heading: By Manufacture Year
items.text: This tab shows two stacked bar charts: IT teams can use this information for capacity planning and lifecycle management.

items.heading: Windows Software Performance
items.text: This tab shows the applications that contribute most to CPU and memory overutilization on Windows devices: Each bar in these charts represents an application and shows the percentage of devices where that application is contributing to high CPU or memory utilization. These insights help IT teams identify high-impact applications and prioritize optimization or upgrades.

items.heading: Mac OS Software Performance
items.text: This tab shows the applications that contribute most to CPU and memory overutilization on Mac OS devices:

items.heading: Introduction
items.text: The Sustainability dashboard in the Analytics module of the HP Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) provides you (IT administrators and stakeholders) with real-time insights into the actual power consumption of your device fleet. This helps organizations to align IT operations with sustainability goals and take measures for optimization. Using the power consumption data, you can prioritize hardware upgrades, enable power-saving settings, forecast IT energy budgets, and support sustainability reporting for corporate Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.

items.heading: Accessing the Sustainability Dashboard
items.text: Field Description Last Signed-In User The name of the user who last logged into the device. Serial Number A unique identifier assigned by the manufacturer. Device Name The name assigned to the device within your organization. Model The name of the device model with specifications. Power Consumption (kWh) Total power consumed, measured in kilowatt-hours. Change Since Last Week (kWh) The difference in energy usage compared to the previous week. Power Measurement The method used - Estimated Consumption (EC) or Power Consumption Measurement (PCM). Manufacturer The name of the device manufacturer. Manufacture Year The year the device was manufactured. Memory The capacity of installed RAM. Active Warranty Indicates if the device is currently under warranty. Location The registered geographical location of the device. Last Seen The date and time the device was last active on the platform. Use the search bar in each field to find devices by Serial number, Device name, Model, and more.

items.heading: Understanding the Sustainability Dashboard
items.text: The dashboard enables you to monitor and analyze power consumption of your fleet and take measures to optimize the power consumption.

items.heading: View Power Consumption Over Time
items.text: A line graph displaying weekly energy usage trends across your fleet. This chart is useful for comparing power usage over 4, 8, or 12-week periods and for identifying anomalies or success in energy-efficiency initiatives. This visualization helps you to monitor consumption patterns and identify optimization opportunities over time by using the following data points:

items.heading: View Power Consumption by Device Models
items.text: This chart displays a ranked list of device models in your fleet based on their total energy consumption. This horizontal bar chart helps you to identify energy-intensive device models, compare consumption patterns across different hardware, and prioritize devices for upgrades, replacement, or optimization. Each entry in the chart includes: Note: Longer bars indicate higher power consumption and shorter bars suggest more energy-efficient models or fewer active devices.

items.heading: View Power Consumption by Location
items.text: A geographical heatmap of energy usage across devices, grouped by their registered locations. This visualization helps you to: The global heatmap with varying shades to represent power usage levels across different regions. In addition you can view:

items.text: The Filters feature in the Sustainability Dashboard allows you to narrow down the power consumption data based on specific device attributes and time periods. This helps you to gain more actionable insights about targeted data set.

items.text: The Driver Inventory report lists driver versions and the devices running each version. This report includes detailed driver-level and device-level insights to support driver compliance and remediation efforts. The data is organized across multiple tabs: Drivers, Drivers Table, and Details.

items.heading: Drivers
items.text: The report show the following columns: This view helps IT teams identify which device models have the most driver-related issues and prioritize remediation.

items.heading: Drivers Table
items.text: This tab provides a detailed list of individual drivers in a tabular format. The default columns include: This view helps IT teams assess specific drivers and track their update status across multiple devices.

items.heading: Details
items.text: This tab provides device-level insights in a table format. The default columns include: This tab helps link driver-related issues to specific devices, users, and operating systems, supporting more effective troubleshooting.

items.heading: Introduction
items.text: The BIOS Inventory report shows the BIOS status of all enrolled devices in your organization. This report is useful to identify devices with outdated BIOS versions and those that need critical updates. Using this data, you (IT administrators) can plan and perform updates to ensure systems are secure and stable. This report also provides detailed, device-specific information, such as BIOS version, device type, and manufacturer. It also shows changes in BIOS status over time and the different BIOS versions used for each device model. The data is displayed across multiple tabs: Summary, Unique BIOS, By Week, and Details.

items.heading: Summary - Reviewing BIOS Compliance
items.text: This tab presents a visual breakdown of BIOS versions by device model. Each bar represents the current BIOS status for a specific model: This visualization enables you to quickly identify device models needing attention and implement measures that enhance system stability and security.

items.heading: Unique BIOS - Understanding BIOS Versions by Model
items.text: This tab displays a bar chart where the X-axis represents the number of unique BIOS versions, and the Y-axis represents the device models. It helps IT teams identify inconsistencies in BIOS updates within the same model and uncover opportunities to standardize BIOS versions across all devices. Hover over a bar to view the different BIOS versions used within that device model.

items.heading: By Week - Access Weekly View of BIOS Update Status
items.text: This tab features a bar chart that monitors changes in BIOS status over time. The X-axis denotes the time period, while the Y-axis indicates the percentage share of the total number of devices. Devices are categorized into four groups: The chart displays the percentage of total devices in each category over time, enabling IT teams in monitoring BIOS compliance trends and facilitating timely interventions.

items.heading: Details - Reviewing Device-Level BIOS Status
items.text: This tab provides detailed information about individual devices and their BIOS status in a tabular format. By default, the following columns are displayed: Field Name Description Last Signed-In User The name or ID of the user who last signed into the device. Serial Number A unique identifier for the device; clickable for additional details. Device Name The assigned name of the device. Device Type The type of device, such as Notebook or Desktop. Device Manufacturer The company that manufactured the device, such as HP or Dell. Device Model The model name or number of the device. Manufacture Date The date when the device was manufactured. Device Platform ID The baseboard identifier of the platform the device is built on. BIOS Manufacturer The company that produced the BIOS firmware. Installed BIOS Version The current BIOS version installed on the device. BIOS Installation Date The date when the current BIOS version was installed. Installed BIOS Status (HP Connect) Indicates whether the installed BIOS is up-to-date, outdated, or requires a critical update, according to HP Connect data. Latest Available BIOS Version (HP Connect) The most recent BIOS version available through HP Connect. Latest Available BIOS Release Date (HP Connect) The release date of the latest BIOS version available via HP Connect. Latest Available BIOS Criticality (HP Connect) Indicates the importance level, such as critical or recommended, of the latest BIOS update from HP Connect. Latest Critical Availability BIOS (HP Connect) The most recent critical BIOS version available through HP Connect. BIOS Remediation by HP Connect Indicates whether BIOS updates can be applied automatically using HP Connect. Remediation Policy The policy governing BIOS update deployment and management. Installed BIOS Status (Softpaq) Indicates whether the installed BIOS is up-to-date, outdated, or requires a critical update, according to Softpaq data. Latest Available BIOS Version (Softpaq) The most recent BIOS version available through Softpaq. Latest Available BIOS Release Date (Softpaq) The release date of the latest BIOS version available via Softpaq. Latest Available BIOS Criticality (Softpaq) Indicates the importance level, such as critical or recommended, of the latest BIOS update from Softpaq. Softpaq Number A unique identifier for the Softpaq package containing the BIOS update. Latest Critical Available BIOS Version (Softpaq) The most recent critical BIOS version available through Softpaq. Latest Critical Available BIOS Version Release Notes (Softpaq) A detailed information of changes included in the latest critical BIOS version from Softpaq. Note: An informational message at the top of the page indicates which BIOS versions are remediable via HP Connect and which require Softpaq-based remediation. Use the search bar within each column to filter data by serial number and device name.

items.heading: Introduction
items.text: The Blue Screen Errors report in the Analytics module provides information about enrolled devices that encountered blue screen and driver crashes. The report also shows the affected models, the main causes, and patterns over time. The data is displayed across following sections or widgets: Note: By default, the data is shown for the last 90 days. You can opt to view data for the last 7, 15, or 30 days. Note: When you apply a date filter to one of the DEX dashboards (System Health, OS Performance, Security, etc.), the DEX Score logic uses the device’s last online date to calculate the score so that it reflects the correct state of the device. However, the BSOD report (and all other reports) uses the selected time range to display data. For example, if a device was online during the first week of the month and had several BSODs, and you apply a 7-day filter to view the DEX Score and the BSOD report for the second week of the month. In this case: This difference in data will be resolved in an upcoming release. The update will ensure that the DEX Score and the BSOD report use consistent logic for the selected time range.

items.heading: View Device-Level BSOD Details
items.text: The lower section of the page provides a detailed, device-level view of Blue Screen of Death (BSOD) incidents in tabular format. By default, the following columns are displayed: Use the search bar available in each field to filter data by serial number, device name, number of occurrences, or date of the last occurrence.

items.heading: Filtering the BSOD Data
items.text: The Filters feature enables you to narrow down BSOD data based on specific criteria, which are grouped into the following three categories: Select the filtering criteria and click Apply to view the BSOD based on the specified criteria.

items.heading: Application Inventory Report
items.text: The Application Inventory report provides a centralized overview of all software applications installed across enrolled devices in the organization, along with the different versions of each application. It also provides detailed information, such as application names, version numbers, publishers, and the number of devices on which each application is installed. This report is useful for IT teams to identify the most frequently used applications, track the number of versions in use, and monitor software distribution across the environment. It supports informed decision-making related to software standardization, version control, license management, migrations, updates, and compliance. The data is displayed through visual charts and tables to provide high-level insights and detailed breakdowns.

items.heading: Top Installed Apps
items.text: This chart displays a horizontal bar chart showing the most commonly installed applications across devices. The chart is sorted by the number of devices where each application is installed.

items.heading: Apps with Most Installed Versions
items.text: This chart displays applications that have the highest number of distinct versions installed across devices. It helps IT teams to identify version sprawl and inconsistencies within the same application. This insight helps IT teams standardize software across the organization by identifying apps with too many coexisting versions and take measures, if required. For example, Outlook for Windows" appears with 31 versions, signaling potential inconsistencies that could affect performance. Click on an application name to view the version distribution for that app.

items.heading: Application Table
items.text: Below the charts is a detailed table view that provides device-level information for each application. The default columns include: Use the gear icon, Column Manager, to manage the columns. This view helps IT teams assess version distribution, detect outdated software, and monitor applications from specific vendors. Use the search bar within each column to filter data by application name, version, or publisher.

items.heading: Filters
items.text: Use the Filters panel to narrow down application data based on device attributes and application details. These filters enable IT teams to perform targeted analysis, simplify troubleshooting, and support version standardization across specific groups of devices. The filter options are grouped into two sections: Devices and Applications: After selecting the criteria, click Apply to update the data displayed on the dashboard.

items.heading: Exporting Data
items.text: Click Export to download the report based on the applied filter criteria. After clicking Export, a popup message appears indicating that the download is in progress and the file will be available in the Notification Center of the platform once ready.

items.heading: Introduction
items.text: The Application Experience report provides insights into application performance across your organization’s devices. It helps you (IT Admin team) to identify problematic apps by tracking crashes, freezes, long load times, and error trends. This data is essential for improving user productivity, minimizing disruptions, and optimizing the digital experience. The data is displayed across two tabs: Installed and Web.

items.heading: Review Performance of Installed App
items.text: This tab shows data about installed desktop applications, including overall application health, crash and freeze trends, and impact on users.

items.heading: Application Score
items.text: This section displays an overall health score based on application crash and freeze incidents. It includes:

items.heading: Apps with Most Crashes
items.text: This section lists the applications that have experienced the most crash incidents. Each row includes the App Name and Crash Count and a horizontal bar for visual comparison. Click on a bar within the chart to view additional information about that app on a new page. The app name is displayed at the top of the page, which provides a deeper analysis of crash and freeze behavior for the selected application. The data is presented across multiple charts and tables: Note: If no data is available, the chart shows a placeholder: "No data to display." Use the search fields at the top of each column to filter or locate specific records.

items.heading: Apps with Most Freezes
items.text: This section lists the applications that have experienced the most freeze incidents. Each row includes the App Name and Crash Count and a horizontal bar for visual comparison. Click on a bar within the chart to view additional information about that app on a new page. The app name is displayed at the top of the new page, which provides a deeper analysis of crash and freeze behavior for the selected application. The data is presented across multiple charts and tables: If no data is available, the chart shows a placeholder: "No data to display."

items.heading: View Performance Table for Desktop Applications
items.text: The lower section of the page table shows a table providing performance insights of individual desktop applications installed across your fleet. It helps you to identify apps that are prone to instability and may require updates or remediation. The columns can be customized using the gear icon (Column Options). Column Description Application Name The name of the installed application. Clickable links allow users to drill down into detailed performance data for that specific app. No. of Crashes Shows the total number of crash incidents recorded for each application across the device fleet. No. of Freezes Indicates the number of times the application became unresponsive (froze) during use. Recommended Version Suggests the most stable version for deployment, based on aggregated performance data from all WXP customer environments. Least Stable Version Indicates the version most associated with crashes or freezes. Helps in identifying and phasing out problematic versions. Impacted Devices Shows the count of devices impacted with the corresponding app.

items.heading: Crashes and Freezes Over Time
items.text: This line chart shows a time-based trend of: The X-axis represents dates, and the Y-axis represents the count. This view helps you monitor trends and assess the impact of stability issues across the fleet. Additionally, you can see an info icon indicating that this chart is not affected by filters.

items.heading: Application-Level Performance Table
items.text: This table shows performance insights of individual desktop applications installed across devices in the organization. This table will help you to identify the apps that are prone to instability and require updates or remediation. This table displays the following columns, which can be controlled using the gear icon - Column Options. Use the search bars located at the top of each column to filter or find specific application data quickly.

items.heading: Review Performance of Browser Apps
items.text: The Web tab focuses on the performance of browser-based applications. It includes metrics, such as page load time and errors.

items.heading: Web Applications Score
items.text: A line chart showing score trends over time, where the X-axis represents the dates and the Y-axis represents the scores. This helps you to identify fluctuations in web application performance and allows timely investigation. Additionally, you can view:

items.heading: Apps with Longest Page Load Time
items.text: This section shows web applications that takes the longest time to load. This helps you to identify apps with poor performance that may impact user experience. Each row shows the App Name and its Average Load Time in Seconds. For example, Dropbox: 194.76 sec

items.heading: Apps with Most Errors
items.text: This section displays the number of errors for each web application, displayed in a descending order. For example: This data helps you to identify high-risk apps that may require attention or vendor follow-up.

items.heading: Apps with Most Errors Over Time
items.text: A line chart where the X-axis shows the timeline (by date) and the Y-axis shows the number of error events. This chart helps you understand error trends for the top web applications with the highest number of recorded errors. Each colored line corresponds to a specific web application, allowing visual comparison of error volume over time. F This chart helps you to identify: Hover over the data points on the chart to view the exact number of errors recorded for each application on a specific date.

items.heading: Web Application Table
items.text: This table at the lower section of the page displays a detailed view of individual web applications.nnEach row represents a web application, and each column presents a specific performance indicator. This table shows the following columns, which you can control using the Gear icon - Column Options. The name of the web application with clickable links. Domain URL The domain through which the application is accessed. For example, docs.google.com or nordvpn.com. Application Score A numerical score representing the overall health of the web application. A red warning icon indicates a critical score. Error Occurrences The total number of error events recorded for the application. This helps to prioritize applications experiencing the most issues. Average Load Time (sec) The average time taken for the application's web page to fully load, measured in seconds. Longer times indicate poor performance. Total Visits The number of user sessions or access instances recorded for the application. A higher number reflects higher usage or engagement. Use the search fields at the top of each column to filter data by application name, domain, score, or any other relevant metric.

items.heading: Introduction
items.text: The OS Performance report in the Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) provides insights into operating system-related issues that affect device stability and user experience. It helps IT teams monitor common problems such as frequent crashes, high CPU usage, and missing updates. This report supports proactive remediation by highlighting performance gaps, recommending corrective actions, and improving the overall health of the device fleet. The data is organized into the following sections:

items.heading: OS Performance Score
items.text: This section provides a high-level overview of operating system stability across the device fleet.

This page helps IT administrators understand which devices fall into the selected performance category and take targeted remediation actions. Details on the page include: Click a recommendation card to view additional details and remediation steps. Column Description Serial Number A unique identifier for each device. Click to view more details. Device Name A user-assigned label of the device. Last Signed-In User The username or ID of the most recent person who logged into the device. Warranty State The current warranty status such as In warranty, Out of warranty, or Unknown. OS Performance Score A numeric score indicating the device’s OS performance. Average CPU Utilization (%) Average CPU usage over the past 30 days. Application CPU Utilization CPU usage attributed to installed applications. Use the search box at the top of each column to filter results by device name, serial number, or other attributes.

items.text: This section highlights the most common OS-related issues in your device fleet. items.heading: Overview of Custom Dashboards and Reports
Introduction
items.text: The Analytics module of the HP Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) provides you (IT administrators) with tailored insights to monitor the performance of your device fleet, identify issues, and make data-driven decisions that improve system reliability and user experience. It includes a set of predefined dashboards and reports with clear visualizations and detailed analytics to support informed decision-making. These insights help improve technical operations and the employee experience. The Analytics feature also allows you to create custom dashboards and modify existing reports, so you can focus on the metrics that are most relevant to your business goals.

items.heading: Overview of the WXP Sustainability Dashboard
Introduction
items.text: The Sustainability Dashboard feature of the Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) offers clear and actionable insights into power consumption across your device fleet. This helps you identify opportunities to enhance sustainability by using the following methods to measure consumption: Only HP PCs with integrated PCM or EC technology are supported for precise power consumption monitoring; unsupported devices are omitted from tracking.

items.heading: Prerequisites
items.text: The WXP power consumption dashboard requires a Workforce Experience Platform Elite subscription. NoteSupported Devices for PCM in WXP: Power Consumption Measurement (PCM) in WXP is supported only on specific device models as listed below. PCM data will not appear on the WXP Sustainability dashboard for devices that do not support this feature. Note Supported Devices for PCM in WXP: Power Consumption Measurement (PCM) in WXP is supported only on specific device models as listed below. PCM data will not appear on the WXP Sustainability dashboard for devices that do not support this feature. items.heading: Introduction
items.text: The Application Experience report provides insights into application performance across your organization’s devices. It helps you (IT Admin team) to identify problematic apps by tracking crashes, freezes, long load times, and error trends. This data is essential for improving user productivity, minimizing disruptions, and optimizing the digital experience. The data is displayed across two tabs: Installed and Web.

items.heading: Review Performance of Installed App
items.text: This tab shows data about installed desktop applications, including overall application health, crash and freeze trends, and impact on users.

items.heading: Application Score
items.text: This section displays an overall health score based on application crash and freeze incidents. It includes:

items.heading: Apps with Most Crashes
items.text: This section lists the applications that have experienced the most crash incidents. Each row includes the App Name and Crash Count and a horizontal bar for visual comparison. Click on a bar within the chart to view additional information about that app on a new page. The app name is displayed at the top of the page, which provides a deeper analysis of crash and freeze behavior for the selected application. The data is presented across multiple charts and tables: Note: If no data is available, the chart shows a placeholder: "No data to display." Use the search fields at the top of each column to filter or locate specific records.

items.heading: Apps with Most Freezes
items.text: This section lists the applications that have experienced the most freeze incidents. Each row includes the App Name and Crash Count and a horizontal bar for visual comparison. Click on a bar within the chart to view additional information about that app on a new page. The app name is displayed at the top of the new page, which provides a deeper analysis of crash and freeze behavior for the selected application. The data is presented across multiple charts and tables: If no data is available, the chart shows a placeholder: "No data to display."

items.heading: View Performance Table for Desktop Applications
items.text: The lower section of the page table shows a table providing performance insights of individual desktop applications installed across your fleet. It helps you to identify apps that are prone to instability and may require updates or remediation. The columns can be customized using the gear icon (Column Options). Column Description Application Name The name of the installed application. Clickable links allow users to drill down into detailed performance data for that specific app. No. of Crashes Shows the total number of crash incidents recorded for each application across the device fleet. No. of Freezes Indicates the number of times the application became unresponsive (froze) during use. Recommended Version Suggests the most stable version for deployment, based on aggregated performance data from all WXP customer environments. Least Stable Version Indicates the version most associated with crashes or freezes. Helps in identifying and phasing out problematic versions. Impacted Devices Shows the count of devices impacted with the corresponding app.

items.heading: Crashes and Freezes Over Time
items.text: This line chart shows a time-based trend of: The X-axis represents dates, and the Y-axis represents the count. This view helps you monitor trends and assess the impact of stability issues across the fleet. Additionally, you can see an info icon indicating that this chart is not affected by filters.

items.heading: Application-Level Performance Table
items.text: This table shows performance insights of individual desktop applications installed across devices in the organization. This table will help you to identify the apps that are prone to instability and require updates or remediation. This table displays the following columns, which can be controlled using the gear icon - Column Options. Use the search bars located at the top of each column to filter or find specific application data quickly.

items.heading: Review Performance of Browser Apps
items.text: The Web tab focuses on the performance of browser-based applications. It includes metrics, such as page load time and errors.

items.heading: Web Applications Score
items.text: A line chart showing score trends over time, where the X-axis represents the dates and the Y-axis represents the scores. This helps you to identify fluctuations in web application performance and allows timely investigation. Additionally, you can view:

items.heading: Apps with Longest Page Load Time
items.text: This section shows web applications that takes the longest time to load. This helps you to identify apps with poor performance that may impact user experience. Each row shows the App Name and its Average Load Time in Seconds. For example, Dropbox: 194.76 sec

items.heading: Apps with Most Errors
items.text: This section displays the number of errors for each web application, displayed in a descending order. For example: This data helps you to identify high-risk apps that may require attention or vendor follow-up.

items.heading: Apps with Most Errors Over Time
items.text: A line chart where the X-axis shows the timeline (by date) and the Y-axis shows the number of error events. This chart helps you understand error trends for the top web applications with the highest number of recorded errors. Each colored line corresponds to a specific web application, allowing visual comparison of error volume over time. F This chart helps you to identify: Hover over the data points on the chart to view the exact number of errors recorded for each application on a specific date.

items.heading: Web Application Table
items.text: This table at the lower section of the page displays a detailed view of individual web applications.nnEach row represents a web application, and each column presents a specific performance indicator. This table shows the following columns, which you can control using the Gear icon - Column Options. The name of the web application with clickable links. Domain URL The domain through which the application is accessed. For example, docs.google.com or nordvpn.com. Application Score A numerical score representing the overall health of the web application. A red warning icon indicates a critical score. Error Occurrences The total number of error events recorded for the application. This helps to prioritize applications experiencing the most issues. Average Load Time (sec) The average time taken for the application's web page to fully load, measured in seconds. Longer times indicate poor performance. Total Visits The number of user sessions or access instances recorded for the application. A higher number reflects higher usage or engagement. Use the search fields at the top of each column to filter data by application name, domain, score, or any other relevant metric.

items.heading: Exporting Data
items.text: Click Export to download the report based on the applied filter criteria. After clicking Export, a popup message appears indicating that the download is in progress and the file will be available in the Notification Center of the platform once ready.

items.heading: Microsoft Teams
items.text: Microsoft Teams is a collaboration platform that supports communication, meetings, file sharing, and app integration in a unified workspace. You can integrate the HP Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) with Microsoft Teams using webhook URLs to receive real-time alerts and notifications directly in Teams channels. This integration helps teams respond faster and work more efficiently.

items.heading: Prerequisites
items.text: Before you start, make sure the following conditions are met: For more information about roles and permissions, see the Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) section in Authentication.

items.heading: Create Webhooks for Teams Channels
items.text: You can create a webhook in any Teams channel where you want to receive WXP alerts. Note: You do not need to be the Teams channel owner. Any channel member can create a webhook.

items.heading: Activate the Microsoft Teams Connector in WXP
items.text: After you’ve created the webhooks, connect WXP to Microsoft Teams.
9.1. Sign in with your Microsoft credentials. 9.2. Authorized users will see the Permissions requested page. Leave the checkbox ‘Consent on behalf of your organization’ unselected. Click Accept.

items.heading: Receiving Alert Notifications in Teams Channels
items.text: After setup, configure WXP alerts to send notifications to Teams channels. Prerequisites: 1. In WXP, go to Alerts Management. 2. Follow the steps in the Configuring Alerts in Workforce Experience Platform guide to add Teams notifications to an alert. 3. When a configured alert is triggered, it will appear in the connected Teams channel. Each Teams alert message includes: Here is an example of a WXP Alert notification in a Teams channel showing battery wear detection details for multiple devices.

items.heading: Introduction
items.text: The Connectors functionality in the HP Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) helps to connect the platform with third-party systems and tools. Connectors act as bridge that support data sharing, process automation, and communication between different systems. This allows organizations to extend the platform’s capabilities while continuing to use their existing tools. Using connectors, organizations can improve compatibility between systems, reduce manual work, and increase efficiency. The platform supports a growing set of pre-built and customizable connectors to support various business needs. Note: Integrations are visible to all Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) users; however, you must be a Connector Admin or Partner Admin to configure and manage integrations.

items.heading: Key Features
items.text: Some of the key features include:

items.heading: Use Cases
items.text: Some of the popular use cases are:

items.heading: Authentication Method
items.text: Azure Active Directory (AAD) authentication is not supported for connector integrations. HPID is the only supported authentication method for API access.

items.heading: API Authentication Requirements
items.text: Best Practice: Create a dedicated service account for API authentication with one of the listed API-enabled roles, separate from the Connector Admin account. Role Can View Integrations Can Connect/Disconnect (UI) Access Documentation API Access Connector Admin Yes No Partner Admin IT Admin Report Admin LDP Admin Support Specialist

items.heading: WXP User Creation
items.text: The Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) user must ensure their email is only associated with one account associated or tenant. If there is a Switch Account option visible when clicking on the Profile icon in the top right corner of WXP, this means a user has access to multiple accounts and you must create a new user.
If a user has access to multiple accounts, please follow the steps below to create a new user under the same account:

items.heading: Power BI
items.text: Important: Before proceeding, please ensure you have followed One User One Account instructions HERE. This ensures you do not experience any authentication conflicts. Power BI is a business intelligence and analytics platform that enables users to connect to data sources, transform raw data into actionable insights, and create interactive reports and dashboards.

items.heading: Power BI Connector
items.text: The Power BI Connector extracts, transforms, and visualizes data in real-time. It creates dynamic reports and dashboards that deliver actionable insights.

items.heading: Supported Operations
items.text: The Power BI Connector supports a range of operations for data extraction and visualization:

items.heading: Key Functionalities
items.text: The Power BI Desktop Connector extracts, cleanses, and prepares data from the HP Workforce Experience (WXP) platform for local analysis in Power BI Desktop. It enables users to work with and visualize data offline before publishing it to the Power BI Service.

items.heading: Integration Workflow
items.text: Note: A Platform Account (HPID) with Report Admin or IT Admin rights is required. Creating a service account is the recommended approach.

items.heading: Prerequisites
items.text: 3.1. Login to https://app.powerbi.com. In the top menu, click the gear icon .
3.2 Under Governance and insights, click Admin Portal. 3.3 In the Admin portal page, go to Tenant Settings.
3.4 Search for Create Workspaces on the right side. 3.5 Under Workspace Settings, click Create Workspaces.
3.6 Expand the Create Workspaces arrow to view two Apply to options.3.7 Select Specific security groups (Recommended as it is less intrusive).
Note: Selecting “The entire organization” is not recommended. 4. Log in to https://portal.azure.com 5. Under Manage Microsoft Entra ID, click View.
5.1 In the Overview page, click Groups.5.2 On the right side, click Add > Group.

6. In the New Group page, under Group type, add a group. (For example, you can add a Group name PowerBIWorkspaceAccessGroup). 6.1 On the right side, under the Add members, select the checkbox to add a designated connection user as a member.

7. In Power BI, go to Tenant settings. 7.1 Under Workspace settings, select the radio button Specific security groups. 7.2 In the Search field, type the group name (e.g., PowerBIWorkspaceAccessGroup) and select it.
7.3 Click Apply.

items.heading: How to Activate
items.text: Note: Ensure you select all necessary reports. Changing the report selection after the connection is established will result in a new Power BI workspace.
4. Click Save.

7.1 Sign in with a Microsoft account and credentials. 7.2 Select the option to grant the user permission to Create and Read datasets. The Power BI integration card displays Connected. The Connect button changes to a Disconnect option.
Note: Allow a few minutes for the reports to appear in Power BI (Note that larger datasets might take longer to process).

items.heading: Power BI Data Connection and Refresh Schedule
Troubleshooting Tips
items.text: 1. Error: "Connection Failed" Cause: A "Connection failed" error occurs when WorkspaceV2 creation is not enabled on the tenant. (Administrator privileges are required to enable this setting.)Solution: Please check step 2 in the Prerequisites section above.

2. No Data Visible in Power BI Cause: Data may not be visible because a user may have access to multiple accounts, which results in a tenant ID mismatch. Solution: Follow the following steps: 1. Verify WXP Portal Account Access Check in the WXP portal if your email is associated with more than one account or tenant. If so, there is a possibility of an authentication conflict, as the system may have authorized against an unintended account.

items.heading: 1.1 Purpose and Scope of the Document
items.text: This document offers a detailed technical design for integrating HP Workforce Experience with ServiceNow's Event and Incident Management modules. It describes the features, architecture, data flow, user interface, and crucial components of the integration, ensuring consistency with ServiceNow’s best practices. This TDD covers the following topics:

items.heading: 1.2 Overview of the Application
items.text: The integration between HP Workforce Experience and ServiceNow enables the real-time processing of device performance and usage data into ServiceNow as events or alerts, which then create incident records. This application is structured into two main sections:

items.heading: 1.3 Target Audience
items.text: This technical design document is aimed at system and application administrators tasked with implementing and managing the HP Workforce Experience integration with ServiceNow’s Event and Incident Management modules. It is also intended for IT operations teams, support engineers, and decision-makers overseeing IT service management processes who seek to utilize this integration to improve their incident response and device management capabilities. Note: ServiceNow integration is available to HP WXP Partners (who can enable it across their managed customer tenants) and to Direct HP WXP customers. Managed customers under a partner account do not have access to ServiceNow integration directly. For more information, please contact HP WXP Support.

items.heading: 2. Key Features and Use Cases
items.text: This integration features a ServiceNow Configuration Wizard designed to streamline the setup and deployment. With its guided, step-by-step interface, the wizard facilitates configuring the connection between HP Workforce Experience and ServiceNow’s Event and Incident management modules. Notable features of the wizard include: The use case diagram below provides a visual representation of the integration from the perspectives of different personas, such as end users and the incident resolvers.

items.heading: 3. Application Architecture
items.text: The Application Architecture section contains the high-level architecture diagram, application scope information, data flow diagrams, and any integrations with other systems/applications. These designs reflect how components of the application interact and how data flows.

items.heading: 4. Data Model
items.text: The Data Model section contains an entity-relationship diagram (ERD), table definitions with field names, types and relationships, and also access control requirements.

items.heading: Introduction
items.text: HP Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) Device Management application allows end-to-end integration between HP Workforce Experience and ServiceNow Configuration Management Database (CMDB). This technical design document presents a detailed description of the application's architecture, data flow, user interface, and configuration requirements. It is designed to be used by system administrators, IT operations professionals, and asset managers who would be deploying, operating, and maintaining the integration for increased visibility of devices, enriching the CI data, and automating incident management in real-time using telemetry and update capabilities. This TDD covers the following topics:

items.heading: Purpose and Scope of the Document
items.text: This document describes the technical design for integrating HP Workforce Experience with ServiceNow's Configuration Management application. It details the features, architecture, data flow, user interface, and crucial components of the integration, ensuring consistency with ServiceNow’s best practices. For installation instructions, a supporting user guide documentation provides comprehensive information on how to install and configure the HP WXP Device Management application.

items.heading: Overview of the Application
items.text: The integration of HP Workforce Experience with ServiceNow’s Configuration Management provides the capability to import ServiceNow CMDB CIs (cmdb_ci_computer) based on HP Workforce Experience Platform data for a specific tenant. The integration enables ongoing CI data enrichment through a pre-defined field mapping, as well on-demand access to telemetry data such as battery status, hardware health, blue screen errors, warranty information, etc. that improves overall incident handling.

items.heading: Target Audience
items.text: This technical design document is aimed at system and application administrators tasked with implementing and managing the HP Workforce Experience integration with ServiceNow’s Configuration Management application. It is also intended for IT operations teams, support engineers, and CMDB and asset managers. Note: ServiceNow integration is available to HP WXP Partners (who can enable it across their managed customer tenants) and to Direct HP WXP customers. Managed customers under a partner account do not have access to ServiceNow integration directly. For more information, please contact HP WXP Support.

items.heading: 2. Key Features and Use Cases
items.text: This integration features a ServiceNow Configuration Wizard designed to streamline the setup and deployment. With its guided, step-by-step interface, the wizard facilitates configuring the connection between HP Workforce Experience and ServiceNow’s Configuration Management application. Notable features of the wizard include: The use case diagram below provides a visual representation of the integration from the perspectives of different personas, such as HP and ServiceNow.

items.heading: Introduction
items.text: The Policies module in the Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) enables IT administrators to enforce configuration standards, security baselines, and compliance rules across managed devices. Without a centralized policy engine, organizations often struggle to maintain alignment across a dynamic and distributed device environment. With Policies, you can define automated system behaviors that maintain a desired configuration state and ensure consistent enforcement without manual intervention. Policies can be applied through device groups, such as static, dynamic, or Entra ID-based, and work in tandem with scripts and remediations to uphold IT governance. Policies act as the enforcement layer for maintaining consistent device configurations and security postures. Integrated into remediation and compliance workflows, they proactively ensure that endpoints remain aligned with corporate standards. This capability helps IT teams reduce risk, maintain compliance, and automate configuration changes at scale. Note: Policies can be edited or deleted only when unassigned.

items.heading: Key Features
items.text: Some of the core capabilities include:

items.heading: FAQs
items.text: 1. Do I need to worry about handling BitLocker with a BIOS Update Policy? A: No. We use Windows Update BIOS update capsules, which automatically handle BitLocker. To be safe, we also add a one-time shutdown script to the device after a BIOS update is applied. This script makes sure BitLocker is suspended before the next restart.

items.heading: Introduction
items.text: The Secrets module in the Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) provides secure storage for credentials used in remediation workflows. It helps IT teams manage sensitive information—such as BIOS passwords and certificates—safely and efficiently. Without this feature, organizations would need to manually handle credentials, increasing the risk of errors and security gaps. With the Secrets feature, you can store and manage admin credentials, certificates, and encryption keys in a centralized, secure location. These secrets are automatically injected into scripts or workflows for a secure execution without exposing sensitive data. Secrets support authentication for BIOS policies and script-based remediations. They also enforce role-based access control (RBAC) and provide audit logs to track usage. This ensures credentials are only used by authorized users, helping teams maintain compliance and improve operational efficiency.

items.heading: Introduction
items.text: The Scripts module in the HP Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) allows you to deploy custom PowerShell 5.1 scripts to groups of managed devices. This feature is currently available for Windows 10 and Windows 11 and helps automate routine administrative tasks across your device fleet. Note: The Scripts feature is currently in Beta. Some advanced capabilities, such as editing and deleting scripts, are planned in future releases. This article provides a step-by-step to the following topics:

items.heading: Accessing Script Library
items.text: Field Description Script Name The name of the script. Version The version number of the script. Created By The user who created the script. Synopsis A brief description of the script. Operation Indicates whether the script is an Action or Evaluation. Optional columns include: Script ID A unique identifier assigned to each script. Run As Indicates the context or user that will run the script. The options are System or User . Host Environment Specifies whether the script runs in a 32-bit or 64-bit environment. Execution Policy Determines script execution rules such as Bypass or Client Default. Use the gear icon (Column Options) to add columns to remove columns from the default view. Click Reset to default to restore the default view.

items.heading: Adding a Script to Library
items.text: Note: Use Client Default to execute signed scripts using a Trusted Publisher. Note: Scripts must be assigned through the Assignments tab. Script editing is currently not supported.

items.heading: Parameter Support in Scripts
items.text: WXP Scripts supports adding input parameters to PowerShell scripts. This feature helps customers and partners create customizable and reusable scripts across their fleet. To assign parameters in a script: The Scripts view displays all parameters that have been set in the script. Parameters must be specified here before creating an assignment. Once created, the assignment wizard show an additional page prompting you to confirm or replace any default values specified for your parameters.

items.heading: View Script Details in Library
items.text: Overview tab Section Fields Script Information Synopsis, Description, Created By, Last Modified Date, and Version Execution Properties Operation Type, Host Environment, Run As, Execution Policy Script Content Displays the raw PowerShell code. Assignments tab Status Current assignment status (e.g., Not Scheduled). Purpose of the script in this context. Action or Evaluation. Assignment Name Name of the deployment instance. Groups Device groups assigned to the script. Activity tab Initiated On Timestamp of script execution. Remediation Name Name of the execution instance. Indicates if the execution was an Action or Evaluation. Initiated By User who triggered the script. Current status (e.g., Completed, In Progress). Use the gear icon (Column Options) to add columns to or remove columns from the default view.

items.heading: Accessing Script Gallery
items.text: From the left menu of WXP, click Remediations > Scripts > Gallery. The Gallery tab is displayed, showing HP-curated script templates. The following columns are displayed: Indicates script availability or status. Last Update Last modification date. Name of the predefined script. Script version number. Author (HP or internal). Operation Type Indicates Action or Evaluation type. Use the gear icon (Column Options) to add or remove columns.

items.heading: Viewing Script Details in the Gallery
items.text: Synopsis, Description, Created By, Last Modified, Version Operation Type, Host Environment, Architecture, Run As, Execution Policy Full PowerShell source code Note: To use a Gallery script, create an assignment and define target groups in the workflow.

items.heading: Accessing Assignments
items.text: From the left menu of WXP, click Remediations > Scripts > Assignments. The Assignments tab is displayed, showing the following details Name of the script assignment. Script Assigned Name of the associated script. Target Groups Author Creator of the assignment. Current assignment status. For example, Scheduled, Not Scheduled. Assignments are required to apply scripts from the Library to devices.

items.text: The new assignment can be viewed on the Assignments tab with a status of Not Scheduled.

items.heading: FAQs
items.text: 1. How long will a script keep trying to run on a device if the device is offline or unreachable? A: WXP will try to run the script for up to 24 hours. After 24 hours, the job is canceled. If the script has a schedule to run again, WXP will try again at the next scheduled time. 2: When a script starts to run, how long will it continue before it times out? A: The default timeout for a script is 20 minutes. You can change this value to any time between 1 and 720 minutes in the script settings.

items.heading: Overview
items.text: Wolf Protect & Trace (P&T) is a security solution enables organizations to locate, lock, and protect devices in real time. With built-in telemetry and integration, it helps IT and security teams to minimize the risk of data exposure when devices are lost, stolen, or misplaced. The solution also supports compliance initiatives by ensuring sensitive data remains secure even if the device is outside corporate control. Protect & Trace with Wolf Connect (P&T/WC) provides the same find, lock and erase functionality, but also supports cellular transport. This allows the users to perform these core functions even when the device is not on the internet, powered down or can’t boot the Operating System, (OS).

https://learn.workforceexperience.hp.com.title: HP WXP - Security and Privacy Technical Document

items.heading: HP WXP - Security and Privacy Technical Document
Introduction
items.text: The HP Workforce Experience Platform (WXP) is a cloud-based enterprise solution that helps IT teams reduce digital friction, enhance security, and proactively resolve device issues. It provides centralized visibility and management of an organization’s PC fleet, such as desktops, laptops, and other connected assets, through a secure, scalable platform. WXP analyzes near real-time telemetry data from devices and applications to identify issues, monitor health, and recommend remediation. IT administrators can view device status, define policies, generate reports, and initiate remote actions to support operational and security needs. This document outlines WXP’s security and privacy architecture, focusing on managed PC devices. It explains how data is collected, transmitted, stored, and protected, and describes the platform’s compliance with data protection standards. The intended audience includes IT administrators, service providers, and security reviewers assessing the platform’s data handling practices.

items.heading: WXP Experience Architecture
items.text: As a one-stop, cloud-based analytics platform for devices, data, and users, WXP incorporates industry-proven, service-level security across its architecture and development processes. Key services include:

items.heading: Users and Roles
items.text: The dashboard of WXP offers multiple types of users and roles, as shown in the following diagram.

items.alt: A diagram of a company AI-generated content may be incorrect.

items.text: Roles Scope Regional Admin This account is used by a business operations administrator at HP to create new tenants for both direct customers and partners. Regional root administrator accounts are controlled and have access to manage and navigate direct customer and partner tenants. Typically, separate root admin accounts exist for each region.Currently, WXP operates in two AWS regional data centers:United States (US West-2), AWS OregonEuropean Union (EU Central-1), AWS Germany This account is used by a business operations administrator at HP to create new tenants for both direct customers and partners. Regional root administrator accounts are controlled and have access to manage and navigate direct customer and partner tenants. Typically, separate root admin accounts exist for each region. Currently, WXP operates in two AWS regional data centers: Partners Partner tenants may include partner admin, service specialist, and sales specialist roles. These tenants can access multiple customer accounts and manage devices, users, and daily operations on their behalf. In addition:A partner admin can invite new users to their organization, add customers, and modify account settings such as branding and support models. They can view and manage customer information and trigger remediation scripts.A service specialist can perform all the tasks of a partner admin, but only at the individual customer level.A sales specialist typically has read-only access to customer information. Partner tenants may include partner admin, service specialist, and sales specialist roles. These tenants can access multiple customer accounts and manage devices, users, and daily operations on their behalf. In addition: HP Support An HP Support account can access multiple companies simultaneously and manage devices, users, and day-to-day operations on behalf of customers for troubleshooting purposes. These actions are managed by HP’s Business Operations team.There are two types of users under an HP Support account: support admins and support specialists. Support admins can create additional support admin or support specialist accounts. An HP Support account can access multiple companies simultaneously and manage devices, users, and day-to-day operations on behalf of customers for troubleshooting purposes. These actions are managed by HP’s Business Operations team. There are two types of users under an HP Support account: support admins and support specialists. Support admins can create additional support admin or support specialist accounts. Company Owner When a company is created, a default Company Owner is assigned by root administrators. By default, this user is also given the IT Admin role to manage users and devices within the company.Company Owners can manage users, handle device-related tasks, and access reports and other administrative functions based on their permissions. While Managed Service Providers (MSPs) manage users across multiple companies, Company Owners independently manage users within their own company. When a company is created, a default Company Owner is assigned by root administrators. By default, this user is also given the IT Admin role to manage users and devices within the company. Company Owners can manage users, handle device-related tasks, and access reports and other administrative functions based on their permissions. While Managed Service Providers (MSPs) manage users across multiple companies, Company Owners independently manage users within their own company. Company User In WXP, a user is an individual within an enterprise company tenant who is assigned a role that allows them to take actions. Users can be created in the following ways:An MSP or company user with the appropriate role can manually create a new user and grant portal access.A user is automatically created during device enrollment (e.g., when enrolling computers or mobile devices) using a company PIN. These users are registered in the portal but may not have immediate access.Company users can be assigned various roles to expand or restrict their access within WXP. In WXP, a user is an individual within an enterprise company tenant who is assigned a role that allows them to take actions. Users can be created in the following ways: IT Admin The IT Admin role is assigned to users within customer or self-managed companies. It allows users to perform key administrative tasks such as managing users and devices. While similar in function to roles available to MSPs, IT Admin access is limited to their own company. Report Admin By default, Company Owners have access to reports. Any company user can also be granted report access. The Report Admin role is read-only and allows users to view information from WXP reports. Connector Admin This role enables secure, role-based management of integrations within WXP. This role governs access to integration actions across all supported connector. It ensures only authorized users can configure and manage integration widgets and connection settings.

items.heading: Key Capabilities
items.text: Connector Admins can perform the following actions within WXP: The following diagram provides a high-level overview of the Insights functionality and capabilities:
